Blog

Video Editor Jobs Blog

A small resource library for the two sides of the market: editors proving fit and creator teams scoping recurring work.

Start here

Choose the question you are trying to answer

Editors Get found for the work you actually want

Portfolio examples, remote job search, rates, outreach, and early editor list guidance.

 Hiring teams Write a brief editors can evaluate

Job descriptions, interview questions, YouTube editor briefs, workflow, budget, and revision process.

 Community Turn repeated questions into better pages

Use Reddit, Facebook, and forum replies to decide which guides and category pages to improve next.

Editors

Portfolio, jobs, outreach, and rates

Editors

Where to find video editor jobs

The best video editor opportunities are split across job boards, private communities, creator teams, agencies, referrals, and local production networks.
Editors and hiring teams

Freelance video editor rates

Rates make more sense when tied to deliverables, source footage, timeline, revision load, and the level of creative ownership required.
Editors

Video editor portfolio examples

A strong portfolio is not one giant reel. It is a small set of matched examples that prove you can handle the exact editing workflow a team needs.

Hiring teams

Briefs, screening, and scope

Hiring teams

How to hire a video editor

The fastest way to hire a strong editor is to write a brief that filters for the actual workflow, not just taste.
Hiring teams

Video editor job description template

Most editing job posts are too vague. This structure helps editors self-select before either side wastes time.
Editors and hiring teams

Freelance video editor rates

Rates make more sense when tied to deliverables, source footage, timeline, revision load, and the level of creative ownership required.
Hiring teams

YouTube video editor job description

YouTube editor roles need more than software requirements. The post should explain channel format, pacing, cadence, references, analytics expectations, and review workflow.

Content queue

Use community replies to decide what to publish next

  • Questions about portfolios should route to portfolio examples, then the editor intake.
  • Questions about vague job posts should route to the job description template, then the hiring intake.
  • Questions about pay should route to pricing and rates guides, then collect clearer budget ranges.
  • Questions about remote work should route to remote pages, then capture timezone, capacity, and software.
